basics.name: Oleksandr Vafin
basics.label: Senior Frontend Developer
basics.summary: Experienced front-end developer with 6+ years crafting innovative, efficient solutions across logistics, telecommunications and SaaS — specializing in Angular with a strong emphasis on performance and usability.
bio: I'm a Senior Frontend Developer based in Prague with over six years of commercial experience building user-centric applications across logistics, telecommunications and SaaS.

My focus is performance and usability — resolving complex bugs, eliminating redundant work, and shaping interfaces that scale. I work primarily in the Angular ecosystem with TypeScript, RxJS, NgRx and Ionic, and I care about code quality, mentorship and a healthy release process.
I've delivered for government tenders, port terminals and telecom operators across four countries — and I enjoy helping teams grow as much as shipping the product.

work.name: Barclays
work.summary: Working in the Treasury department at Barclays, participating in the development of an OpenFin-based platform and integrating existing applications into it with shared context.
highlights: Implemented OpenFin notifications
Added Intent events and integrated them into the top-bar menu for cross-app communication
Created a shared-components app to reduce repeated code across applications and ensure consistency
Developed and improved UI components in a shared UI library, from gathering requirements to releasing, documenting, and supporting the features

work.name: Neoris
work.summary: Working on enterprise-level frontend applications with focus on performance, reliability and team growth.
highlights: Resolved complex bugs and optimized performance, reducing the number of requests on key pages by up to 30%
Provided mentorship and onboarding support, reducing time to first closed task from 3 weeks to 6 days
Successfully launched an Ionic application for a new region, achieving 100+ users in the first month
Participated in the software release process, ensuring code quality and deployment readiness

work.name: Port 443
work.summary: Contributed to cross-functional team discussions and delivered frontend improvements on an existing application.
highlights: Implemented a new version of table component, significantly reducing codebase size and improving application speed
Collaborated on epics and user stories, ensuring features met client and business requirements
Increased test coverage of the application up to 85%

work.name: Friendly
work.summary: Designed and maintained user interfaces for scalable web applications with emphasis on responsive design and performance.
highlights: Developed a high-performance application for a telecommunication company to manage IoT devices across 1,000+ towers
Improved stability by resolving memory leaks and eliminating redundant API requests
Built responsive UIs with strong emphasis on enhanced user experience

work.name: PPL 33-35
work.summary: Delivered critical frontend projects for port terminal and government clients under tight deadlines.
highlights: Migrated core legacy application from Angular 4 to Angular 14
Delivered an MVP for a government tender in a tight timeframe
Developed a robust solution for a port terminal operating seamlessly for over five years

work.name: Vugasi
work.summary: Led full-stack development of an MVP video/text chat platform with integrated multiplayer games.
highlights: Designed and implemented a video/text chat application integrated with simple multiplayer games using WebRTC
Led end-to-end development of the MVP, owning both frontend and backend
Utilized Angular, WebRTC, Node.js, MongoDB, and PostgreSQL
